etymology.description: 病(病) (bìng, "illness") + 得(得) (de, "structural particle") + 很(很) (hěn, "very") + 重(重) (zhòng, "heavy") together form the phrase, indicating a severe state of illness.
etymology.interpretation: 病(病) (bìng, "illness") refers to a state of being unwell. 得(得) (de, "structural particle") is used after an adjective to form a complement indicating the degree or manner of the preceding action or state. 很(很) (hěn, "very") intensifies the adjective following it. 重(重) (zhòng, "heavy") conveys the seriousness or gravity of the condition. Together, '病得很重' literally describes an illness that is 'very heavy,' metaphorically indicating a severe or serious illness.

examples.chinese: 他病得很重(他病得很重)，需要住院治疗(需要住院治療)。
examples.pinyin: Tā bìng de hěn zhòng, xūyào zhùyuàn zhìliáo.
examples.english: He is seriously ill and needs to be hospitalized for treatment.

examples.chinese: 医生说她病得很重(醫生說她病得很重)，必须马上送去急诊(必須馬上送去急診)。
examples.pinyin: Yīshēng shuō tā bìng de hěn zhòng, bìxū mǎshàng sòng qù jízhěn.
examples.english: The doctor said she is seriously ill and must be sent to the emergency room immediately.

examples.chinese: 他的父亲病得很重(他的父親病得很重)，因此家人非常担心(因此家人非常擔心)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de fùqīn bìng de hěn zhòng, yīncǐ jiārén fēicháng dānxīn.
examples.english: His father is seriously ill, so the family is very worried.
